mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a three-month window signals an imminent recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real GDP growth indicate synchronized consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a GDPC1-only baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, indicating the interaction adds no marginal predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production following a supply shock indicates capacity normalization, which eases input cost inflation and supports equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows negative correlation with forward SPX returns during supply-constrained periods, or the signal reverses direction when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5% annually forces market pricing of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward SPX returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL crosses the 2.5% threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales demonstrates broad-based economic expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly differ from periods with payroll growth alone.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarterly momentum in real GDP growth directly accelerates corporate earnings revision cycles, mechanically translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: GDPC1 momentum shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across three consecutive business cycles, invalidating the earnings-revision transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained Federal funds rate increase above 2.0% during a tightening cycle raises corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from baseline during periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, or the signal flips to positive during liquidity-driven rallies.
